items.title: Global optimization: parallelism, budget strategy, optional GUI suspension
dod: parallel execution policy documented
token/context budget strategy documented and enforceable

items.title: Telegram support bot task-oriented dialogue guardrails
dod: remove generic chitchat fallback and enforce task-entry first sentence
only reference previous project context on explicit continuation intent
each customer-facing turn includes one concrete next action or constrained option set
items.notes: User requested MD-driven execution to shift from generic assistant-style chatting to professional task-oriented support dialogue.

items.title: Fixed 10-step workflow hardening with triplet pre-verify guard
dod: execution order is fixed to 10-step with mandatory analysis/find before plan and plan before implementation
local check/contrast/fix loop is explicit and requires triplet guard contract tests before final verify
completion requires evidence for connected plus accumulated plus consumed
items.notes: Hardens workflow contracts and executable gates to prevent docs-only or prompt-only completion for integration defects.

items.title: Single-purpose, single-flow, single-responsibility operating model refactor
dod: repo purpose truth source is singular and explicit
canonical execution flow truth source is singular and explicit
current task truth fields are mandatory and gate-enforced
items.notes: Consolidates purpose/flow/task/runtime truth ownership and narrows subsystem docs to non-competing roles.

items.title: Scaffold dual-source mode with controlled live-reference export
dod: scaffold and scaffold-pointcloud support source-mode template or live-reference with template as default
live-reference export is manifest-whitelisted, path-safe, auditable, and records source commit plus source mode metadata
generated projects keep CTCP contract chain compatibility and verify through scripts/verify_repo entrypoint
items.notes: Adds a controlled live-reference exporter without breaking existing template scaffolding behavior.

items.title: Text-first frontend control plane with single CTCP execution bridge
dod: frontend text control plane routes greeting and smalltalk away from project planning while preserving PM-style project handling
frontend execution mutations and run-state queries go through scripts/ctcp_front_bridge.py bridge capabilities only
tests cover greeting isolation, detailed requirement understanding, duplicate-question prevention, leakage guard, single-bridge invocation path, and non-contradictory visible state
items.notes: Implements phase 1-2 frontend behavior and wiring while preserving future realtime extension boundaries.

items.title: Markdown object state machine baseline for process governance
dod: introduce markdown object registry and state machine docs as single truth sources
land minimal active object set for process plus strategy plus rule with explicit IDs and states
forbid direct delete path and require inheritance check policy before object promotion or replacement
items.notes: Adds stateful markdown governance baseline to prevent silent deletion and hidden legacy behavior drift.

items.title: Support and production-agent shared librarian+whiteboard collaboration loop
dod: dispatcher writes production agent requests and outcomes into shared whiteboard state and links librarian retrieval hints
all production provider prompts include whiteboard snapshot and librarian clues so roles can consume same context
support whiteboard and production dispatch reuse one artifact path to avoid context split between customer support and execution agents
items.notes: Extends existing support-side whiteboard+librarian loop to production dispatch path so all execution agents share one collaboration board.

items.title: Unify customer-visible support replies behind a humanized notice gate
dod: all customer-visible report, bundle, dispatch, fallback, and write-fail notices go through the same natural support reply gate and do not leak internal labels or raw exceptions
smalltalk, capability, and thanks turns without an active run stay local and do not create or mutate project execution state
support-bot fallbacks remain customer-facing even when model/provider execution fails, with regression coverage for direct-leak paths and dataset expectations
items.notes: Removes remaining mechanical/internal customer-facing wording by routing all visible notices and local smalltalk paths through the same natural reply contract.

items.title: Align Telegram-created runs with actually available execution provider
dod: Telegram greeting-only turns remain local, but project-intake run creation no longer defaults into a broken external API path when external API env is not truly ready
api_agent readiness rejects ollama-style placeholder key without OPENAI_BASE_URL instead of waiting for runtime 401
regression coverage proves Telegram-created runs downgrade execution dispatch away from api_agent under placeholder-key env and still preserve provider-selection contracts
items.notes: Prevents Telegram-created runs from inheriting a broken api_agent path when the environment only contains an ollama placeholder key.

items.title: Shift support reply design goal to boundary-driven expression
dod: team-mode docs state the design goal as letting the mechanical layer decide boundaries while the agent decides phrasing
support-lead prompt and support-bot defaults constrain leakage, actionability, and question count without forcing a fixed customer reply structure
regression coverage proves the support fallback and default prompt stay customer-facing and boundary-driven
items.notes: Replaces the fixed three-part support-reply design goal with a boundary-first contract that leaves phrasing to the agent.

items.title: Validate CTCP markdown-scoped frontend edits with a local capability reply change
dod: CURRENT.md explicitly scopes a frontend change instead of treating markdown contracts as a blanket block on frontend edits
frontend capability questions stay local and can explicitly mention frontend-safe change scope without entering project planning or bridge mutation
regression coverage plus canonical verify prove the scoped frontend path still passes CTCP gates
items.notes: Uses a small frontend reply change to prove markdown contracts only constrain scope and process; they do not forbid frontend code edits when CURRENT.md binds the lane explicitly.

items.title: Enforce backend hard-local role boundaries while keeping shared whiteboard available
dod: dispatch provider resolution no longer remaps librarian/context_pack or contract_guardian/review_contract away from local_exec via mode, role_providers, or CTCP_FORCE_PROVIDER
shared whiteboard context remains available to execution roles while local librarian stays usable for deterministic repo-scoped lookup/context_pack generation
regression coverage proves hard-local role enforcement and whiteboard-assisted provider prompts stay connected
items.notes: Backend hard-local role change and S16 fixture alignment are complete; final canonical verify closure was finished by the follow-on support-bot humanization blocker task.

items.title: Clear the remaining support-bot humanization verify blocker without widening backend scope
dod: bare project creation / new-run kickoff replies ask for project goal, input, and expected result instead of echoing a generic project-creation request
project-detail reply shaping rewrites low-signal or internal-marker raw replies into frontend-reviewed project-manager replies that prefer the current detailed requirement source
targeted support-bot/frontend regressions and canonical verify clear the remaining python unit-test blocker

tests: python -m py_compile tools/telegram_cs_bot.py frontend/response_composer.py tests/test_support_bot_humanization.py tests/test_frontend_rendering_boundary.py

items.notes: Cleared the remaining support-bot humanization regressions by tightening bare project kickoff fallback and falling back from low-signal/internal-marker raw project replies to frontend-reviewed PM replies.

items.title: Repair support-bot provider connectivity and customer-facing fallback closure
dod: support reply provider candidates treat manual_outbox as a real fallback and consume deferred provider states instead of dropping to model_unavailable
ollama_agent serves support replies through native Ollama chat so local support sessions no longer fail on OpenAI-compatible chat 400
api_agent and openai_responses_client no longer let OPENAI_API_KEY=ollama mask real local-notes credentials for external API mode
targeted regressions, triplet guards, live smoke, and canonical verify produce auditable evidence

tests: python -m py_compile scripts/ctcp_support_bot.py tools/providers/api_agent.py tools/providers/ollama_agent.py scripts/externals/openai_responses_client.py tests/test_support_bot_humanization.py tests/test_api_agent_templates.py tests/test_openai_responses_client_resilience.py tests/test_ollama_agent.py

items.notes: Repairs the real support-reply provider chain observed in production-like Telegram/support sessions by closing deferred fallback handling, local Ollama support chat wiring, placeholder credential precedence, and live restart/verify closure.

items.title: Wire support bot into front bridge, shared whiteboard, and librarian-backed project flow
dod: support_bot project-like turns create or bind a CTCP run through scripts/ctcp_front_bridge.py instead of staying in a support-only reply loop
support_bot records project turns into the bound run's shared whiteboard and consumes whiteboard plus librarian context through bridge-safe runtime helpers
support_bot customer replies are grounded in real run status and bridge-returned decision state without bypassing CTCP runtime contracts
regression coverage plus canonical verify prove the support entrypoint is connected to backend flow, accumulated in issue memory, and consumed by downstream dispatch/whiteboard paths

tests: python -m py_compile scripts/ctcp_support_bot.py scripts/ctcp_front_bridge.py scripts/ctcp_dispatch.py tests/test_support_bot_humanization.py tests/test_runtime_wiring_contract.py

items.notes: Turns scripts/ctcp_support_bot.py from a provider-only support shell into a bridge-safe frontend entry that can create/bind runs, consume shared whiteboard context, and reflect librarian-backed backend progress.

items.title: Isolate support memory zones and lock explicit API support routing away from local semantic drift
dod: support session state separates persistent project brief, per-turn memory, and provider runtime buffer so low-signal follow-ups do not overwrite the active project brief
conversation mode detection classifies the current turn without letting prior high-signal history force a fresh PROJECT_INTAKE on short follow-ups
greeting or smalltalk turns stay on a local customer-facing fast path and explicit api_agent override no longer falls through to ollama_agent on reply-shaping issues
regression coverage plus canonical verify prove the support entrypoint preserves project memory, avoids wrong pause semantics, and keeps fallback behavior customer-facing
items.notes: Fixes the live support-session defect where project brief memory, current-turn memory, and provider fallback state bleed together, producing wrong English greeting, project-brief overwrite, and api->ollama semantic drift.

items.title: Route all customer-visible support turns through the support model and align foundational docs
dod: GREETING and SMALLTALK remain classified outside project planning logic, but customer-visible replies for those turns are generated through the configured support model instead of a local fast path
support-bot runtime no longer emits `local_smalltalk` replies during normal execution; explicit model routing still avoids semantic drift into a different provider on strict override
authoritative support lane docs and prompt contract describe all customer-visible support turns as model-routed with only failure degradation allowed outside the model path
targeted regressions plus canonical verify prove greeting turns now consume the provider path while project-memory isolation and backend bridge behavior remain intact
items.notes: Implements the explicit product decision that all user-visible support replies, including greetings and smalltalk, must be model-authored; docs and runtime should no longer claim or use a local greeting fast path.

items.title: Repair support API Chinese reply encoding across subprocess, wrapper, and public reply gates
dod: api_agent no longer corrupts Chinese model output when Windows child-process stdout is emitted in a local codepage instead of UTF-8
default external OpenAI wrapper scripts explicitly use UTF-8 stdio so support reply provider output stays stable under the normal Python-based API path
support bot public reply shaping suppresses mojibake-style garbage text as a last-resort safety net instead of leaking it to Telegram users
targeted regressions, triplet guards, live smoke, and canonical verify leave auditable evidence for the fixed support entrypoint

tests: python -m py_compile tools/providers/api_agent.py scripts/externals/openai_agent_api.py scripts/externals/openai_plan_api.py scripts/externals/openai_patch_api.py scripts/ctcp_support_bot.py tests/test_api_agent_templates.py tests/test_support_bot_humanization.py

items.notes: Fixed the live Telegram mojibake by hardening api_agent subprocess decoding, pinning the default OpenAI wrapper scripts to UTF-8 stdio, adding a support-bot mojibake safety net, and closing with live smoke plus canonical verify evidence.

items.title: Prevent support turns from overwriting project goals, dropping execution directives, or overpromising blocked execution
dod: project goal memory no longer gets overwritten by implementation-detail turns such as Windows or Qt6 preference updates, and those details are stored in a separate isolated memory zone

when a support session is already bound to a run, execution-directive turns such as asking for a first draft stay on the project path and continue to record through the backend bridge instead of falling to SMALLTALK

customer-visible replies for blocked or decision-needed backend states are grounded in real runtime state and no longer preserve optimistic raw provider promises about already starting the first version
targeted regressions, triplet guards, workflow gate, issue memory update, and canonical verify leave auditable evidence for connected plus accumulated plus consumed

tests: python -m py_compile scripts/ctcp_support_bot.py frontend/conversation_mode_router.py frontend/response_composer.py tests/test_support_bot_humanization.py tests/test_runtime_wiring_contract.py

items.notes: Closed the support state-grounding defect by isolating project goal vs. technical constraints vs. execution directives, keeping bound-run execution follow-ups on the backend path, grounding blocked-state customer replies in real runtime truth, and verifying through regressions plus canonical verify.

items.title: Add progressive support-to-production path tests
dod: a progressive test suite proves at a simple level that the bridge writes support turns into production artifacts such as support_frontend_turns.jsonl and support_whiteboard.json
the same suite proves at a medium level that support context is read back from RUN.json, frontend_request.json, and support_whiteboard.json instead of chat memory

the complex path proves scripts/ctcp_support_bot.py can create or bind a run, record the support turn, advance through the real bridge helpers, and still land a customer-visible support_reply artifact
targeted regressions, triplet guards, workflow gate, and canonical verify leave auditable evidence for connected plus consumed support-to-production coverage
items.notes: Adds an explicit simple-to-complex proof that the customer-facing support entrypoint really reaches production run artifacts through the bridge without changing runtime semantics, and closes with targeted regressions plus canonical verify evidence.

items.title: Make support replies API-first with local-only degradation and no canned fallback shell
dod: support bot defaults customer-visible replies to api_agent instead of ollama_agent or manual_outbox-style canned fallback
when api_agent is unreachable or returns unusable reply payload, support bot degrades only to a local provider and explicitly tells the user the API path is unavailable
customer-visible fallback no longer uses the old fixed '暂时还没连上稳定的回复能力' or '收到，我先帮你整理一下' shell
targeted regressions, triplet guards, workflow gate, and canonical verify leave auditable evidence for connected plus accumulated plus consumed
items.notes: Runtime changes and targeted regressions landed, but canonical verify is currently blocked by preexisting out-of-scope untracked files under generated_projects/ triggering patch_check scope failure.

items.title: Deliver CTCP-style scaffold packages from support instead of thin placeholder project dirs
dod: when support package delivery is requested and the current project source is only a thin placeholder directory, support bot materializes an external CTCP-style scaffold package instead of zipping the placeholder as-is
the scaffold package is generated outside the repo via the existing scaffold entrypoint and sent through the Telegram document delivery path
customer-visible support replies and delivery context describe the package structure truthfully as a CTCP-style scaffold rather than inventing feature-complete project contents
targeted regressions, triplet guards, workflow gate, and canonical verify leave auditable evidence for connected plus accumulated plus consumed
items.notes: Support delivery now materializes an external CTCP-style scaffold package for thin placeholder project dirs and the targeted regressions passed, but canonical verify remains blocked by the preexisting out-of-scope repo-local generated_projects tree under the current PLAN scope gate.

items.title: Contractize task-progress dialogue, test showcase chain, and version metadata truth
dod: task-progress dialogue rules are authoritative, testable, and bound to execution state rather than style-only guidance

quality-gate docs define response lint, test showcase acceptance, and metadata consistency acceptance for contract verification

items.title: Land Persona Test Lab contracts, isolated session rules, and regression assets
dod: production assistant, test user personas, and judge scoring are contractually separated
every persona case requires a fresh session and writes standardized run artifacts outside the repo

quality gates and artifact contracts define persona regression acceptance and anti-pollution boundaries

items.title: Simplify agent contracts into thin root AGENTS, single 5-step flow, and minimal local overrides
dod: root AGENTS.md becomes a short agent main contract with Purpose, Single Entry, Single Flow, Allowed Questions, Required Outputs, Non-Negotiable Rules, and Routing
